content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
May 20, 1997
John E. Cavanaugh
City Attorney
City of Cypress
Law Offices of Woodruff, Spradlin & Smart
701 South Parker Street, Suite 7000
Orange, California 92868-4720
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 1-96-347
Dear Mr. Cavanaugh:
You requested advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions ofthe Political Reform
Act (the “Act”)’ on behalfofMayor Pro Tern Mary Ann Jones.
Mayor Pro Tern Jones is the vice president and manager ofthe Huntington Beach Branch
Office of Sanwa Bank California (“Sanwa Bank”). Sanwa Bank California is the wholly owned
subsidiary of Sanwa Bank Limited, which is one of the largest banks worldwide with assets of
over $523 billion. Although Sanwa Bank California is not publicly traded on the American
Stock Exchange or the New York Stock Exchange, Sanwa Bank Limited is publicly traded on the
Tokyo, Osaka, and Kyoto Domestic Stock Exchanges as well as London, Paris, and Franlcfurt.
Mayor Pro Tern Jones is a salaried employee of Sanwa Bank and does not work on a commission
basis.
On April 17, 1997, we had a lengthy discussion regarding your request. I informed you
that your questions were very general in nature and were not linked to any pending governmental
decisions. However, we discussed all ofyour questions and how to analyze them under the
conflict-of-interest provisions. Since Ms. Jones is a salaried employee of the bank, with no
ownership interest, the focus is whether a decision will have a material financial effect upon
Sanwa Bank. To analyze the material financial effect of a decision on Sanwa Bank, please refer
to Regulation 18702.2 (d) or (e), copy enclosed. Since we cannot issue a formal advice letter
based on the questions posed, you agreed to withdraw your request for advice.
Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109
-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
42X I STRFFT
cIlTTI Rnn

flO1A
In1’ ,,-‘


File No. 1-96-347
Page 2
On May 8, 1997, I spoke to Mayor Pro Tern Jones and explained the foregoing to her.
I have also enclosed copies of advice letters that deal with similar situations, which you
may use for guidance.
Ifyou have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
S
f Counsel, Legal Division
SGC:JS:jlw
Enclosures:
Barker Advice Letter, No. 1-91-071
Williamson Advice Letter, No. 1-92-628
Williams Advice Letter, No. 1-92-5 18
Lopez Advice Letter, No. A-95-063
Cauchois Advice Letter, No. A-95-233
Regulation 18702.2

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Mayor Pro Tem Mary Ann Jones, as a salaried vice president and manager of a bank branch, faces conflict-of-interest issues under the Political Reform Act when the bank may be affected by city decisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC could not issue a formal advice letter because the questions were too general and not linked to pending governmental decisions. However, the FPPC explained that the analysis should focus on whether any decision would have a material financial effect on Sanwa Bank, and directed the requester to Regulation 18702.2(d) or (e) for guidance.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter where the requester agreed to withdraw the request after the April 17, 1997 discussion. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections exist in the document; the substance appears in the body of the letter. Document dated May 20, 1997 (later than the 1996 file year indicated).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Mayor Pro Tem Mary Ann Jones, as a salaried vice president and manager of a bank branch, faces conflict-of-interest issues under the Political Reform Act when the bank may be affected by city decisions.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC could not issue a formal advice letter because the questions were too general and not linked to pending governmental decisions. However, the FPPC explained that the analysis should focus on whether any decision would have a material financial effect on Sanwa Bank, and directed the requester to Regulation 18702.2(d) or (e) for guidance.

embedding.summary: The FPPC's General Counsel declined to issue a formal advice letter regarding Mayor Pro Tem Jones's potential conflicts of interest as a salaried bank employee, as the questions posed were too general and not tied to specific pending decisions. The FPPC provided guidance on how to analyze material financial effects under the Political Reform Act and enclosed similar advice letters for reference.
